title: rubus rhombifolius subsp. sampaioanus
intro: Rubus rhombifolius subsp. sampaioanus is a rare subspecies of the rhombifolius raspberry, a type of wild raspberry plant native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its high content of ellagic acid and other polyphenols. The health benefits of Rubus rhombifolius subsp. sampaioanus include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its ability to act as an antimicrobial agent. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
